key_insight: ZO-Act turns zeroth-order fine-tuning into optimization over a small activation-derived low-rank coefficient space, which lowers estimator variance and finite-difference error without backpropagation.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical way to adapt large and quantized language models under memory or autodiff constraints while keeping trainable parameters lightweight and optimizer-friendly.
method: Compute a compact activation basis once at initialization for each linear layer.
Freeze model weights and optimize only low-rank coefficient matrices using forward-only loss evaluations.
Use the restricted subspace to support low-bit fine-tuning and momentum-based optimizers like Adam.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent improvements over strong ZO baselines on Llama-3-8B, OPT-13B, and INT4 Llama-3-8B across language understanding, QA, and commonsense reasoning.
watch_item: Performance depends on how well the fixed activation subspace matches the task, so a poor subspace choice can introduce approximation bias and limit gains.
paper_title: ZO-Act: Efficient Zeroth-Order Fine-Tuning via One-Shot Activation-Informed Low-Rank Subspaces
